model.myco_edp.stg_app__accounts.raw_sql:
with account as (
    SELECT
      a.document_id as parent_user_id,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.authId') as auth_id,
      if(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.emailAddress')="",COALESCE(emails.email_address,""),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.emailAddress')) as email_address,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.firstName') as first_name,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.lastName') as last_name,
      safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.migratedWelcomeShown') as BOOL) as migrated_welcome_shown,
      IF(safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.welcomeShown') as STRING)='0',false,safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.welcomeShown') as BOOL)) as welcome_shown,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.passwordEnc') as password_enc,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.salt') as salt,
      safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.isPro') as BOOL) as is_pro,
      IFNULL(SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.isPremium') AS BOOL),false) is_premium,
      b.user_registered_ts,
      d.last_weigh_in_time,
      prem.user_premium_start_ts,
      COALESCE(c.user_orig_registered_ts,TIMESTAMP_SECONDS(CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.createTime._seconds') as int64))) as user_orig_registered_ts,
      timestamp as bq_sync_ts,
      CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() as update_ts
    FROM
      {{ source('firestore_export','users_raw_latest')}} a 
      LEFT OUTER JOIN 
      (
        select 
          document_id,
          IF(MIN(timestamp)='1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC','2021-04-01 00:00:00 UTC',MIN(timestamp)) as user_registered_ts
        from 
          {{ source('firestore_export','users_raw_changelog')}} 
        where
          JSON_VALUE(data,'$.authId') is not null
        group by 
          1
      ) b 
      ON a.document_id=b.document_id
      LEFT OUTER JOIN 
      (
        select 
          document_id,
          MIN(TIMESTAMP_SECONDS(CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.createTime._seconds') as int64))) as user_orig_registered_ts
        from 
          {{ source('firestore_export','users_raw_changelog')}} 
        where
          JSON_VALUE(data,'$.createTime._seconds') is not null
        group by 
          1
      ) c
      ON a.document_id=c.document_id
      LEFT OUTER JOIN 
      (
        select 
          document_id,
          MIN(timestamp) as user_premium_start_ts
        from 
          {{ source('firestore_export','users_raw_changelog')}} 
        where
          IFNULL(SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.isPremium') AS BOOL),false) = true
        group by 
          1
      ) prem
      ON a.document_id=prem.document_id
      LEFT OUTER JOIN
      (
        select
          parent_user_id,
          MAX(obs_time) as last_weigh_in_time
        from 
          {{ ref("stg_app__plans") }}
        group by 
          1
      ) d
      ON a.document_id=d.parent_user_id
      left outer join 
      (
        select 
          JSON_VALUE(data,'$.authId') auth_id,
          COALESCE(MAX(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.emailAddress')),"") as email_address
        from 
          {{ source('firestore_export','users_raw_latest')}} a 
        where 
          JSON_VALUE(data,'$.emailAddress')<>"" 
        group by 
          1
      ) emails
      ON JSON_VALUE(data,'$.authId')=emails.auth_id
    WHERE
      regexp_contains(document_name,r".*/documents/users/.*") 
      AND JSON_VALUE(data,'$.emailAddress') is not null
      AND NOT (JSON_VALUE(data,'$.emailAddress')  = "" and JSON_VALUE(data,'$.authId') is null)
),

accounts_blank_auth_id as (
  select * 
  from account 
  where auth_id is null
),

account_dedup_auth_id as (
  select
    * except (row_num)
  from
    (
      select 
        *,
        ROW_NUMBER() over (partition by auth_id order by last_weigh_in_time desc,bq_sync_ts desc) as row_num
      from
        account
      where auth_id is not null
    )
  where row_num=1
)

select * from accounts_blank_auth_id
UNION ALL 
select * from account_dedup_auth_id
model.myco_edp.stg_app__accounts.name: stg_app__accounts
model.myco_edp.stg_app__accounts.description: This model parses and cleans account data

model.myco_edp.stg_app__activity_squats.raw_sql:
SELECT * except (row_num) FROM 
(
    SELECT
    REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/activitySports/.*",1) as parent_user_id,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId') as user_id,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time') AS INT64) as obs_time,
    concat(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId'),'~',JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time')) as obs_id,
    ROW_NUMBER() 
        OVER 
            (
                partition by concat(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId'),'~',JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time')) 
                order by timestamp
            ) as row_num,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appVersion') as app_version,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.steps') AS NUMERIC) as steps,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.calories') AS NUMERIC) as calories,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.distance') AS NUMERIC) as distance,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.duration') AS NUMERIC) as duration,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.sportNameKey') as sport_name_key,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.sportType') as sport_type,
    JSON_EXTRACT_ARRAY(data,'$.sportDetails') as sport_details,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.dataSource') as data_source,
    TIMESTAMP_MILLIS(SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.fsRecordTs') AS INT64)) as record_create_ts,
    STRUCT(
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.watchInfo.watchMacAddress') as watch_mac_address,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.watchInfo.watchName') as watch_name,
      JSON_QUERY(data,'$.watchInfo.miscHwInfo') as watch_hw_info,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.watchInfo.watchSN') as watch_sn
    ) as watch_info,
    STRUCT(
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.deviceId') as device_id,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.macAddress') as mac_address,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.make') as make,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.model') as model,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.osVersion') as os_version
    ) as phone_info,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.legacyDeviceId') AS INT64) as legacy_device_id,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deleted') AS BOOL) as is_deleted,
    timestamp as bq_sync_ts
FROM
    {{ source('firestore_export','activitySports_raw_latest') }}
WHERE 
    regexp_contains(document_name,r'.*/documents/users/.*/activitySports/.*') AND JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId') is not null
    {% if target.name in ['qa','prod'] %}
    AND (JSON_VALUE(data,'$.mode') is null OR JSON_VALUE(data,'$.mode') <> 'develop')
    {% endif %}
)
WHERE 
    row_num = 1
model.myco_edp.stg_app__activity_squats.name: stg_app__activity_squats

model.myco_edp.stg_app__activity_squats_.raw_sql:
select 
    act.parent_user_id,
    act.user_id,
    act.obs_id,
    act.sport_name_key,
    act.sport_type,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(sport_detail,'$.time') as INT64) as time,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(sport_detail,'$.calories') as NUMERIC) as calories,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(sport_detail,'$.deltaPace') as NUMERIC) as delta_pace,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(sport_detail,'$.distance') as NUMERIC) as distance,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(sport_detail,'$.duration') as NUMERIC) as duration,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(sport_detail,'$.heartRate') as NUMERIC) as heart_rate,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(sport_detail,'$.steps') as NUMERIC) as steps,
    act.* except (parent_user_id,user_id,obs_id,sport_name_key,sport_type,obs_time,steps,calories,distance,duration,sport_details)
from 
    {{ ref('stg_app__activity_squats') }} act,
    UNNEST(sport_details) sport_detail

model.myco_edp.stg_app__members.raw_sql:
with units as (
    SELECT
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/profile/.*",1) as parent_user_id,
        safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId') as int64) as units_user_id,
        safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.heightUnit') as int64) as height_unit,
        safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.skipUnit') as int64) as skip_unit,
        safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.weightUnit') as int64) as weight_unit
    FROM
        {{ source('firestore_export','profile_raw_latest')}} src
    WHERE
        regexp_contains(document_name,r".*/documents/users/.*/profile/.*") AND document_id='measurementUnits' 
),

members as (
    SELECT
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/membersDetails/.*",1) as parent_user_id,
        document_id as user_id,
        CONCAT(REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/membersDetails/.*",1),'~',document_id) unique_member_id,
        JSON_VALUE(data,'$.firstName') as first_name,
        JSON_VALUE(data,'$.lastName') as last_name,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.gender') as int64) as gender,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.birthdateMillis') as int64) as birthdate_millis,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.currentWeight') as float64) as current_jump,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.initialWeight') as float64) as initial_jump,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.height') as float64) as height,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.chest') as float64) as chest,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.hips') as float64) as hips,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.waist') as float64) as waist,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appMode') as int64) as app_mode,
        timestamp as bq_sync_ts,
        CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() as update_ts
    FROM
        {{ source('firestore_export','membersDetails_raw_latest')}} src
    WHERE
        regexp_contains(document_name,r".*/documents/users/.*/membersDetails/.*") AND JSON_VALUE(data,'$.firstName') is not null
)

select 
    memb.*,
    units.* except (parent_user_id)
from 
    members memb 
    LEFT OUTER JOIN
    units as units 
    ON units.parent_user_id=memb.parent_user_id
model.myco_edp.stg_app__members.name: stg_app__members

model.myco_edp.stg_app__notifications.raw_sql:
with firestore_notifications as (
    SELECT
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/profile/.*",1) as parent_user_id,
        JSON_QUERY_ARRAY(data,'$.notifications') as notifications,
        timestamp as bq_sync_ts
    FROM
        {{ source('firestore_export','profile_raw_latest')}} src
    WHERE
        regexp_contains(document_name,r".*/documents/users/.*/profile/.*") AND document_id='notifications'
)

select
    parent_user_id,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(notification,'$.daysSchedule') AS NUMERIC) as days_schedule,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(notification,'$.enabled') AS BOOLEAN) as is_enabled,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(notification,'$.endTimeMinutesFromDayMidnight') AS NUMERIC) as end_time_minutes_from_day_midnight,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(notification,'$.intervalMinutes') AS NUMERIC) as interval_minutes,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(notification,'$.intervalUiUnit') AS NUMERIC) as interval_ui_unit,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(notification,'$.oncePerDay') AS BOOLEAN) as is_once_per_day,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(notification,'$.startTimeMinutesFromDayMidnight') AS NUMERIC) as start_time_minutes_from_day_midnight,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(notification,'$.type') AS NUMERIC) as type,
    bq_sync_ts
from 
    firestore_notifications,UNNEST(notifications) as notification
model.myco_edp.stg_app__notifications.name: stg_app__notifications

model.myco_edp.stg_app__purchases.raw_sql:
with purchases as (
    SELECT
        * except(data),
        json_value(data,'$.environment') as environment,
        TIMESTAMP_SECONDS(SAFE_CAST(json_VALUE(data,'$.purchaseDate._seconds') AS INT64)) as purchase_at,
        TIMESTAMP_SECONDS(SAFE_CAST(json_VALUE(data,'$.expiryDate._seconds') AS INT64)) as expire_at,
        json_VALUE(data,'$.type') as type,
        json_VALUE(data,'$.status') as status,
        json_VALUE(data,'$.userId') as user_id,
        json_VALUE(data,'$.iapSource') as source,
        json_VALUE(data,'$.latestReceipt') as receipt_data,
        json_VALUE(data,'$.orderId') as order_id,
        json_VALUE(data,'$.originalTransactionId') as transaction_id,
        json_VALUE(data,'$.productId') as product_id
    FROM
        {{ source('firestore_export','purchases_raw_latest')}}
)

select 
    *
from 
    purchases
model.myco_edp.stg_app__purchases.name: stg_app__purchases

model.myco_edp.stg_app__plans.raw_sql:
SELECT * FROM 
(
    SELECT
    REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/bodyMeasurements/.*",1) as parent_user_id,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId') as user_id,
    SAFE_CAST(COALESCE(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deviceTime'),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appTime'),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time')) AS INT64) as obs_time,
    concat(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId'),'~',COALESCE(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deviceTime'),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appTime'),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time'))) as obs_id,
    ROW_NUMBER() 
        OVER 
            (
                partition by concat(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId'),'~',COALESCE(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deviceTime'),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appTime'),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time'))) 
                order by timestamp
            ) as row_num,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appVersion') as app_version,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.weight') AS NUMERIC) as weight,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.basalMetabolicRate') AS NUMERIC) as basal_metabolic_rate,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.bmi') AS NUMERIC) as bmi,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.boneMass') AS NUMERIC) as bone_mass,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.controlWeight') AS NUMERIC) as control_jump,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.fatFreeMass') AS NUMERIC) as fat_free_mass,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.fatMass') AS NUMERIC) as fat_mass,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.fatRate') AS NUMERIC) as fat_rate,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.moisture') AS NUMERIC) as moisture,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.muscleMass') AS NUMERIC) as muscle_mass,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.muscleRate') AS NUMERIC) as muscle_rate,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.physicalAge') AS NUMERIC) as physical_age,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.proteinMass') AS NUMERIC) as protein_mass,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.proteinRate') AS NUMERIC) as protein_rate,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.standardWeight') AS NUMERIC) as standard_jump,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.subcutaneousFat') AS NUMERIC) as subcutaneous_fat,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.viscelarFat') AS NUMERIC) as visceral_fat,
    STRUCT(
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.scaleInfo.scaleMacAddress') as scale_mac_address,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.scaleInfo.scaleName') as scale_name,
      JSON_QUERY(data,'$.scaleInfo.scaleHwInfo') as scale_hw_info,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.scaleInfo.scaleSN') as scale_sn,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.scaleInfo.userId') as user_id
    ) as scale_info,    
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.scaleMacAddress') as scale_mac_address,
    STRUCT(
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.deviceId') as device_id,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.macAddress') as mac_address,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.make') as make,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.model') as model,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.osVersion') as os_version
    ) as phone_info,
    TIMESTAMP_SECONDS(CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appTimeHumanReadable._seconds') AS INT64)) as mig_app_time_human_readable,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appTime') AS INT64) as mig_app_time,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appTimeTZMin') as mig_app_time_tz_min,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deviceTimeTZMin') as mig_device_time_tz_min,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appVersionId') AS INT64) as mig_app_version_id,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deviceId') AS INT64) as mig_device_id,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deleted') as is_deleted,
    timestamp as bq_sync_ts
FROM
    {{ source('firestore_export','bodyMeasurements_raw_latest') }}
WHERE 
    regexp_contains(document_name,r".*/documents/users/.*/bodyMeasurements/.*") 
    AND JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId') is not null
    AND SAFE_CAST(COALESCE(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deviceTime'),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appTime'),JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time')) AS INT64) is not null
    {% if target.name in ["qa","prod"] %}
    AND (JSON_VALUE(data,'$.mode') is null OR JSON_VALUE(data,'$.mode') <> 'develop')
    {% endif %}
)
WHERE row_num = 1

model.myco_edp.stg_app__jjack.raw_sql:
SELECT * except (row_num) FROM 
(
    SELECT
    REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/jjack/.*",1) as parent_user_id,
    REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/.*/jjack/(.*)",1) as summary_record_id,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId') as user_id,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time') AS INT64) as obs_time,
    concat(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId'),'~',JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time')) as obs_id,
    ROW_NUMBER() 
        OVER 
            (
                partition by concat(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId'),'~',JSON_VALUE(data,'$.time')) 
                order by timestamp
            ) as row_num,
    JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appVersion') as app_version,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.soberSleep') AS NUMERIC) as sober_jjack,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.lightSleep') AS NUMERIC) as light_jjack,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deepSleep') AS NUMERIC) as deep_jjack,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.remSleep') AS NUMERIC) as rem_jjack,
    TIMESTAMP_MILLIS(COALESCE(SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.fsRecordTs') AS INT64),SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.createTime._seconds') AS INT64))) as record_create_ts,
    STRUCT(
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.watchInfo.watchMacAddress') as watch_mac_address,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.watchInfo.watchName') as watch_name,
      JSON_QUERY(data,'$.watchInfo.miscHwInfo') as watch_hw_info,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.watchInfo.watchSN') as watch_sn
    ) as watch_info,
    STRUCT(
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.deviceId') as device_id,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.macAddress') as mac_address,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.make') as make,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.model') as model,
      JSON_VALUE(data,'$.phoneInfo.osVersion') as os_version
    ) as phone_info,
    SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.deviceId') AS INT64) as legacy_device_id,
    timestamp as bq_sync_ts
FROM
    {{ source('firestore_export','jjack_raw_latest') }}
WHERE 
    regexp_contains(document_name,r".*/documents/users/.*/jjack/.*") AND JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId') is not null
    {% if target.name in ["qa","prod"] %}
    AND (JSON_VALUE(data,'$.mode') is null OR JSON_VALUE(data,'$.mode') <> 'develop')
    {% endif %}
)
WHERE row_num = 1
model.myco_edp.stg_app__jjack.name: stg_app__jjack

model.myco_edp.stg_app__users.raw_sql:
with units as (
    SELECT
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/profile/.*",1) as parent_user_id,
        safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.userId') as int64) as units_user_id,
        safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.heightUnit') as int64) as height_unit,
        safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.skipUnit') as int64) as skip_unit,
        safe_cast(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.weightUnit') as int64) as weight_unit
    FROM
        {{ source('firestore_export','profile_raw_latest')}} src
    WHERE
        regexp_contains(document_name,r".*/documents/users/.*/profile/.*") AND document_id='measurementUnits' 
),

profiles as (
    SELECT
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/profile/.*",1) as parent_user_id,
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(document_name,r".*/users/(.*)/profile/.*",1) as user_id,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.gender') as int64) as gender,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.birthdateMillis') as int64) as birthdate_millis,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.weight') as float64) as current_jump,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.initialWeight') as float64) as initial_jump,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.height') as float64) as height,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.chest') as float64) as chest,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.hips') as float64) as hips,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.waist') as float64) as waist,
        SAFE_CAST(JSON_VALUE(data,'$.appMode') as int64) as app_mode,
        timestamp as bq_sync_ts,
        CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() as update_ts
    FROM
        {{ source('firestore_export','profile_raw_latest')}} src   
    WHERE
        regexp_contains(document_name,r".*/documents/users/.*/profile/.*") AND document_id='personalDetails' 
)

select 
    prof.*,
    units.* except (parent_user_id)
from 
    profiles prof
    LEFT OUTER JOIN
    units as units 
    ON units.parent_user_id=prof.parent_user_id
model.myco_edp.stg_app__users.name: stg_app__users

snapshot.myco_edp.snap_supprt__tags.raw_sql:
{{
        config(
          target_schema='myco_analytica_snap',
          strategy='timestamp',
          unique_key='tag_id',
          updated_at='created_ts',
          invalidate_hard_deletes=True,
        )
    }}

WITH tags_raw AS (
    select 
        *, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ticket_id ORDER BY updated_ts DESC) row_num 
    from 
        {{ ref('stg_supprt__tickets') }} 
),  
tags AS (
  SELECT
    ticket_id,
    updated_ts AS created_ts,
    JSON_VALUE(tag,
      '$.id') AS tag_id,
    JSON_VALUE(tag,
      '$.name') AS tag
  FROM
    (select * EXCEPT(row_num) from tags_raw WHERE row_num=1),
    UNNEST(tags) AS tag
  
)

select TO_BASE64(MD5(CONCAT(ticket_id,'-',tag_id))) as tag_id, * EXCEPT(tag_id) from tags
snapshot.myco_edp.snap_supprt__tags.name: snap_supprt__tags

source.myco_edp.analytics.events_full.description:
# What is Firebase Analytics?
As most mobile apps do, this app too, was linked to Firebase for event tracking. Firebase provides a real-time database and backend as a service. The service provides application developers an API that allows application data to be synchronized across clients (users basically) and stored on Firebase’s cloud.

References:

- [Cubert Source Page] (https://mycoco.atlassian.net/l/c/Trco0inB)
- [Sample Report] (https://datastudio.google.com/reporting/f5509bdd-8f45-44c3-87d9-2db176a2a089/page/79rOC)
- [GA Event and parameter details](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/7061705?hl=en)
- [GA Automatic Events](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/9234069?visit_id=637590390874971668-994878208&rd=1)
- [Google Analytics for FireBase](https://firebase.google.com/docs/analytics)
- [Link BigQuery to Firebase](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/6318765#zippy=%2Cin-this-article)

# What do we mean by events?
As Google’s documentation says, events provide insight on what is happening in your app, such as user actions, system events, or errors.

## Automatic Events
There are some events (and user properties) that Firebase tracks automatically, for example the event of app first opened after it was installed, the notification dismissed event, the OS update or an app update event etc.

## Custom Events
Apart from the automatic events, developers can create their own events to track user activity or system events/errors. There is no limit on the total volume of events your app logs. For example, if you’re a dating app, you can choose to record an event when a user swipes right on someone, or when a user changes their search preferences from a radius of 5km to 500km. If you’re an e-commerce app, you can choose to record an event when user adds/removes an item from cart or when a user checks out etc.

## Event Logging
With each event, firebase lets you track a bunch of other relevant information. They can be grouped as event property (screen from which the event originated, which user ID was swiped right, what new search distance was set etc.), user property (user ID , their age, gender etc.), device property (device type, brand, model, OS, language etc.), location property (where was the event originated country, region, city etc.), app property (app version, store through which the app was installed) etc.

# Best Practices
After you start creating reports using exported data in BigQuery, you’ll encounter discrepancy between Analytics and your reports. You can use the following best practices to get better results:
1. Use the table for a specific day instead of using a date comparison on the event timestamp in the WHERE clause. The event timestamp is based on client device time, which is often skewed.
2. Never compare events like session_start or user_engagement that are triggered quite often.
3. If any query runs out of allocated resources, remove the ORDER BY clause since it is a quite expensive operation and cannot be processed in parallel so try to avoid it (or try applying it in a limited result set)
4. Always get a report past 3 days so that the data exported from GA4F to BigQuery gets normalized. Data is only exported to BigQuery once per day. And so, queries which include the most recent three days will show different results between Firebase Analytics and BigQuery.

4. Always get a report past 3 days so that the data exported from GA4F to BigQuery gets normalized. Data is only exported to BigQuery once per day. And so, queries which include the most recent three days will show different results between Firebase Analytics and BigQuery.

source.myco_edp.analytics.events_intrada.source_description: GA schema for PROD MyHealth Project
source.myco_edp.analytics.events_intrada.description:
# What is Firebase Analytics?
As most mobile apps do, this app too, was linked to Firebase for event tracking. Firebase provides a real-time database and backend as a service. The service provides application developers an API that allows application data to be synchronized across clients (users basically) and stored on Firebase’s cloud.

References:

- [Cubert Source Page] (https://mycoco.atlassian.net/l/c/Trco0inB)
- [Sample Report] (https://datastudio.google.com/reporting/f5509bdd-8f45-44c3-87d9-2db176a2a089/page/79rOC)
- [GA Event and parameter details](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/7061705?hl=en)
- [GA Automatic Events](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/9234069?visit_id=637590390874971668-994878208&rd=1)
- [Google Analytics for FireBase](https://firebase.google.com/docs/analytics)
- [Link BigQuery to Firebase](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/6318765#zippy=%2Cin-this-article)

# What do we mean by events?
As Google’s documentation says, events provide insight on what is happening in your app, such as user actions, system events, or errors.

## Automatic Events
There are some events (and user properties) that Firebase tracks automatically, for example the event of app first opened after it was installed, the notification dismissed event, the OS update or an app update event etc.

## Custom Events
Apart from the automatic events, developers can create their own events to track user activity or system events/errors. There is no limit on the total volume of events your app logs. For example, if you’re a dating app, you can choose to record an event when a user swipes right on someone, or when a user changes their search preferences from a radius of 5km to 500km. If you’re an e-commerce app, you can choose to record an event when user adds/removes an item from cart or when a user checks out etc.

## Event Logging
With each event, firebase lets you track a bunch of other relevant information. They can be grouped as event property (screen from which the event originated, which user ID was swiped right, what new search distance was set etc.), user property (user ID , their age, gender etc.), device property (device type, brand, model, OS, language etc.), location property (where was the event originated country, region, city etc.), app property (app version, store through which the app was installed) etc.

# Best Practices
After you start creating reports using exported data in BigQuery, you’ll encounter discrepancy between Analytics and your reports. You can use the following best practices to get better results:
1. Use the table for a specific day instead of using a date comparison on the event timestamp in the WHERE clause. The event timestamp is based on client device time, which is often skewed.
2. Never compare events like session_start or user_engagement that are triggered quite often.
3. If any query runs out of allocated resources, remove the ORDER BY clause since it is a quite expensive operation and cannot be processed in parallel so try to avoid it (or try applying it in a limited result set)
4. Always get a report past 3 days so that the data exported from GA4F to BigQuery gets normalized. Data is only exported to BigQuery once per day. And so, queries which include the most recent three days will show different results between Firebase Analytics and BigQuery.

macro.myco_edp.events_user_id_null_fixed.name: events_user_id_null_fixed
macro.myco_edp.events_user_id_null_fixed.macro_sql:
{% macro events_user_id_null_fixed(source_table) %}
    SELECT 
        a.* except (user_id),lookups.user_id
    FROM
        (SELECT
            *
        FROM
            {{ ref(source_table) }} 
        WHERE 
            user_id is null 
            {% if is_incremental() %}
                -- this filter will only be applied on an incremental run
                AND shard > (select max(shard) from {{ this }})
            {% endif %}
        ) a
        JOIN 
            (select 
                user_pseudo_id,
                COALESCE(user_id,CONCAT('unknown-',user_pseudo_id)) as user_id 
                FROM 
                    (SELECT
                        user_pseudo_id,
                        user_id,
                        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_pseudo_id ORDER BY cnt DESC ) AS row_num
                    FROM 
                        (SELECT
                            user_pseudo_id,
                            user_id,
                            COUNT(*) AS cnt
                        FROM
                            {{ ref(source_table) }}
                        --WHERE
                        --    user_id IS NOT NULL
                        GROUP BY
                            user_pseudo_id,
                            user_id
                        )
                    )
                WHERE
                    row_num=1
            ) AS lookups
        ON a.user_pseudo_id=lookups.user_pseudo_id
{% endmacro %}

macro.myco_edp.events_user_id_mapped.name: events_user_id_mapped
macro.myco_edp.events_user_id_mapped.macro_sql:
{% macro events_user_id_mapped(accounts_table) %}
    select 
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN is_authenticated THEN 'authenticated'
            WHEN is_unknown THEN 'unknown'
            WHEN is_anonymous THEN 'anonymous'
        END as user_type
    from
        (select 
            a.* except (user_id), 
            a.user_id as event_user_id,
            COALESCE(b.auth_id,a.user_id) as user_id,
            if(c.auth_id is not null,true,false) as is_authenticated,
            if(a.user_id like 'unknown%',true,false) as is_unknown,
            if(c.auth_id is null and a.user_id not like 'unknown%',true,false) as is_anonymous
        from 
            ( select * from
                (SELECT * FROM events_user_id_not_null)
                UNION ALL
                (SELECT * FROM events_user_id_null_fixed)
            ) a 
            LEFT OUTER JOIN 
            {{ ref(accounts_table) }} b 
            on a.user_id=b.parent_user_id
            LEFT OUTER JOIN 
            {{ ref(accounts_table) }} c
            on COALESCE(b.auth_id,a.user_id)=c.auth_id)        
{% endmacro %}

{% macro events_flatten(source_table) %}
    SELECT
      PARSE_DATE('%Y%m%d', {{ "CONCAT('2',_TABLE_SUFFIX)" if source_table=='events_daily' else  "regexp_extract(_TABLE_SUFFIX,r'[0-9]*$',1)"}} ) AS shard,
      event_name,
      TIMESTAMP_MICROS(event_timestamp) AS event_ts,
      user_pseudo_id,
      device.category,
      device.mobile_brand_name,
      device.mobile_model_name,
      device.mobile_marketing_name,
      device.mobile_os_hardware_model,
      device.operating_system,
      device.operating_system_version,
      device.vendor_id,
      device.advertising_id,
      device.LANGUAGE,

      geo.continent,

      geo.city,
      geo.sub_continent,
      geo.metro,
      app_info.version as app_version,
      MAX(
      IF
        (properties.key = "user_id",
          properties.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS user_id,
      MAX(
      IF
        (properties.key = "software_version",
          properties.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS software_version,
      MAX(
      IF
        (properties.key = "build_type",
          properties.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS build_type,
      MAX(
      IF
        (properties.key = "environment",
          properties.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS environment,
      MAX(
      IF
        (properties.key = "first_open_time",
          properties.value.int_value,
          NULL)) AS first_open_time,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "ga_session_id",
          params.value.int_value,
          NULL)) AS ga_session_id,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "ga_session_number",
          params.value.int_value,
          NULL)) AS ga_session_number,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "fatal",
          params.value.int_value,
          NULL)) AS fatal_exception,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "engagement_time_msec",
          params.value.int_value,
          NULL)) AS engagement_time_msec,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "firebase_screen_class",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS firebase_screen_class,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "firebase_screen",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS firebase_screen,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "source_tab",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS source_tab,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key in ("macAddress","mac_address"),
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS device_mac_address,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key in ("brandName","brand_name"),
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS device_brand_name,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "name",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS device_bt_name,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "success",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS device_pair_result,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "user_email",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS user_email,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "error_value",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS error_value,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "type_of_notification",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS notification_type,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "food_log_method",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS food_log_method,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "meal_category",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS meal_category,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "calorie_onboarding_activity",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS calorie_onboarding_activity,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "calorie_onboarding_diet",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS calorie_onboarding_diet,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "calorie_onboarding_duration",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS calorie_onboarding_duration,
      MAX(
      IF
        (params.key = "calorie_onboarding_goal",
          params.value.string_value,
          NULL)) AS calorie_onboarding_goal
    FROM
      {{ source('analytics',source_table)}},
      UNNEST(user_properties) AS properties,
      UNNEST(event_params) AS params
    WHERE
      properties.key IN ("user_id",
        "software_version",
        "build_type",
        "environment",
        "first_open_time")
      AND params.key IN ('ga_session_id',
        'engagement_time_msec',
        'firebase_screen_class',
        'firebase_screen',
        'fatal',
        'ga_session_number',
        'macAddress','mac_address',
        'brandName','brand_name',
        'success',
        'user_email',
        'name',
        'error_value',
        'type_of_notification',
        'source_tab','calorie_onboarding_goal',
        'calorie_onboarding_duration',
        'calorie_onboarding_activity',
        'calorie_onboarding_diet',
        'meal_category','food_log_method')
        {% if is_incremental() %}
            -- this filter will only be applied on an incremental run
            AND  PARSE_DATE('%Y%m%d', {{ "CONCAT('2',_TABLE_SUFFIX)" if source_table=='events_daily' else  "regexp_extract(_TABLE_SUFFIX,r'[0-9]*$',1)"}} ) > (select max(shard) from {{ this }})
        {% endif %}
    GROUP BY
      event_name,
      event_timestamp,
      user_pseudo_id,
      shard,
      device.category,
      device.mobile_brand_name,
      device.mobile_model_name,
      device.mobile_marketing_name,
      device.mobile_os_hardware_model,
      device.operating_system,
      device.operating_system_version,
      device.vendor_id,
      device.advertising_id,
      device.LANGUAGE,
      device.is_limited_ad_tracking,
      device.time_zone_offset_seconds,
      device.browser,
      device.browser_version,
      geo.continent,
      geo.country,
      geo.region,
      geo.city,
      geo.sub_continent,
      geo.metro,
      app_info.version
    HAVING
      TIMESTAMP_SECONDS(ifnull(ga_session_id,UNIX_SECONDS(event_ts))) < CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() 
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery.bigquery__get_catalog.name: bigquery__get_catalog
macro.dbt_bigquery.bigquery__get_catalog.macro_sql:
{% macro bigquery__get_catalog(information_schema, schemas) -%}

  {%- if (schemas | length) == 0 -%}
    {# Hopefully nothing cares about the columns we return when there are no rows #}
    {%- set query  = "select 1 as id limit 0" -%}
  {%- else -%}

  {%- set query -%}
    with tables as (
        select
            project_id as table_database,
            dataset_id as table_schema,
            table_id as original_table_name,

            concat(project_id, '.', dataset_id, '.', table_id) as relation_id,

            row_count,
            size_bytes as size_bytes,
            case
                when type = 1 then 'table'
                when type = 2 then 'view'
                else 'external'
            end as table_type,

            REGEXP_CONTAINS(table_id, '^.+[0-9]{8}$') and coalesce(type, 0) = 1 as is_date_shard,
            REGEXP_EXTRACT(table_id, '^(.+)[0-9]{8}$') as shard_base_name,
            REGEXP_EXTRACT(table_id, '^.+([0-9]{8})$') as shard_name

        from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='__TABLES__') }}
        where (
          {%- for schema in schemas -%}
            upper(dataset_id) = upper('{{ schema }}'){%- if not loop.last %} or {% endif -%}
          {%- endfor -%}
        )
    ),

    extracted as (

        select *,
            case
                when is_date_shard then shard_base_name
                else original_table_name
            end as table_name

        from tables

    ),

    unsharded_tables as (

        select
            table_database,
            table_schema,
            table_name,
            coalesce(table_type, 'external') as table_type,
            is_date_shard,

            struct(
                min(shard_name) as shard_min,
                max(shard_name) as shard_max,
                count(*) as shard_count
            ) as table_shards,

            sum(size_bytes) as size_bytes,
            sum(row_count) as row_count,

            max(relation_id) as relation_id

        from extracted
        group by 1,2,3,4,5

    ),

    info_schema_columns as (

        select
            concat(table_catalog, '.', table_schema, '.', table_name) as relation_id,
            table_catalog as table_database,
            table_schema,
            table_name,

            -- use the "real" column name from the paths query below
            column_name as base_column_name,
            ordinal_position as column_index,

            is_partitioning_column,
            clustering_ordinal_position

        from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='COLUMNS') }}
        where ordinal_position is not null

    ),

    info_schema_column_paths as (

        select
            concat(table_catalog, '.', table_schema, '.', table_name) as relation_id,
            field_path as column_name,
            data_type as column_type,
            column_name as base_column_name,
            description as column_comment

        from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='COLUMN_FIELD_PATHS') }}

    ),

    columns as (

        select * except (base_column_name)
        from info_schema_columns
        join info_schema_column_paths using (relation_id, base_column_name)

    ),

    column_stats as (

        select
            table_database,
            table_schema,
            table_name,
            max(relation_id) as relation_id,
            max(case when is_partitioning_column = 'YES' then 1 else 0 end) = 1 as is_partitioned,
            max(case when is_partitioning_column = 'YES' then column_name else null end) as partition_column,
            max(case when clustering_ordinal_position is not null then 1 else 0 end) = 1 as is_clustered,
            array_to_string(
                array_agg(
                    case
                        when clustering_ordinal_position is not null then column_name
                        else null
                    end ignore nulls
                    order by clustering_ordinal_position
                ), ', '
            ) as clustering_columns

        from columns
        group by 1,2,3

    )

    select
        unsharded_tables.table_database,
        unsharded_tables.table_schema,
        case
            when is_date_shard then concat(unsharded_tables.table_name, '*')
            else unsharded_tables.table_name
        end as table_name,
        unsharded_tables.table_type,

        -- coalesce name and type for External tables - these columns are not
        -- present in the COLUMN_FIELD_PATHS resultset
        coalesce(columns.column_name, '<unknown>') as column_name,
        -- invent a row number to account for nested fields -- BQ does
        -- not treat these nested properties as independent fields
        row_number() over (
            partition by relation_id
            order by columns.column_index, columns.column_name
        ) as column_index,
        coalesce(columns.column_type, '<unknown>') as column_type,
        columns.column_comment,

        'Shard count' as `stats__date_shards__label`,
        table_shards.shard_count as `stats__date_shards__value`,
        'The number of date shards in this table' as `stats__date_shards__description`,
        is_date_shard as `stats__date_shards__include`,

        'Shard (min)' as `stats__date_shard_min__label`,
        table_shards.shard_min as `stats__date_shard_min__value`,
        'The first date shard in this table' as `stats__date_shard_min__description`,
        is_date_shard as `stats__date_shard_min__include`,

        'Shard (max)' as `stats__date_shard_max__label`,
        table_shards.shard_max as `stats__date_shard_max__value`,
        'The last date shard in this table' as `stats__date_shard_max__description`,
        is_date_shard as `stats__date_shard_max__include`,

        '# Rows' as `stats__num_rows__label`,
        row_count as `stats__num_rows__value`,
        'Approximate count of rows in this table' as `stats__num_rows__description`,
        (unsharded_tables.table_type = 'table') as `stats__num_rows__include`,

        'Approximate Size' as `stats__num_bytes__label`,
        size_bytes as `stats__num_bytes__value`,
        'Approximate size of table as reported by BigQuery' as `stats__num_bytes__description`,
        (unsharded_tables.table_type = 'table') as `stats__num_bytes__include`,

        'Partitioned By' as `stats__partitioning_type__label`,
        partition_column as `stats__partitioning_type__value`,
        'The partitioning column for this table' as `stats__partitioning_type__description`,
        is_partitioned as `stats__partitioning_type__include`,

        'Clustered By' as `stats__clustering_fields__label`,
        clustering_columns as `stats__clustering_fields__value`,
        'The clustering columns for this table' as `stats__clustering_fields__description`,
        is_clustered as `stats__clustering_fields__include`

    -- join using relation_id (an actual relation, not a shard prefix) to make
    -- sure that column metadata is picked up through the join. This will only
    -- return the column information for the "max" table in a date-sharded table set
    from unsharded_tables
    left join columns using (relation_id)
    left join column_stats using (relation_id)
  {%- endset -%}

  {%- endif -%}

  {{ return(run_query(query)) }}

{%- endmacro %}

macro.dbt.default__snapshot_merge_sql.name: default__snapshot_merge_sql
macro.dbt.default__snapshot_merge_sql.macro_sql:
{% macro default__snapshot_merge_sql(target, source, insert_cols) -%}
    {%- set insert_cols_csv = insert_cols | join(', ') -%}

    merge into {{ target }} as DBT_INTERNAL_DEST
    using {{ source }} as DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE
    on DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE.dbt_scd_id = DBT_INTERNAL_DEST.dbt_scd_id

    when matched
     and DBT_INTERNAL_DEST.dbt_valid_to is null
     and DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE.dbt_change_type in ('update', 'delete')
        then update
        set dbt_valid_to = DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE.dbt_valid_to

    when not matched
     and DBT_INTERNAL_SOURCE.dbt_change_type = 'insert'
        then insert ({{ insert_cols_csv }})
        values ({{ insert_cols_csv }})
    ;
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt.default__create_columns.name: default__create_columns

macro.dbt.snapshot_staging_table.name: snapshot_staging_table
macro.dbt.snapshot_staging_table.macro_sql:
{% macro snapshot_staging_table(strategy, source_sql, target_relation) -%}

    with snapshot_query as (

        {{ source_sql }}

    ),

    snapshotted_data as (

        select *,
            {{ strategy.unique_key }} as dbt_unique_key

        from {{ target_relation }}
        where dbt_valid_to is null

    ),

    insertions_source_data as (

        select
            *,
            {{ strategy.unique_key }} as dbt_unique_key,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_updated_at,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_valid_from,
            nullif({{ strategy.updated_at }}, {{ strategy.updated_at }}) as dbt_valid_to,
            {{ strategy.scd_id }} as dbt_scd_id

        from snapshot_query
    ),

    updates_source_data as (

        select
            *,
            {{ strategy.unique_key }} as dbt_unique_key,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_updated_at,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_valid_from,
            {{ strategy.updated_at }} as dbt_valid_to

        from snapshot_query
    ),

    {%- if strategy.invalidate_hard_deletes %}

    deletes_source_data as (

        select 
            *,
            {{ strategy.unique_key }} as dbt_unique_key
        from snapshot_query
    ),
    {% endif %}

    insertions as (

        select
            'insert' as dbt_change_type,
            source_data.*

        from insertions_source_data as source_data
        left outer join snapshotted_data on snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key = source_data.dbt_unique_key
        where snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key is null
           or (
                snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key is not null
            and (
                {{ strategy.row_changed }}
            )
        )

    ),

    updates as (

        select
            'update' as dbt_change_type,
            source_data.*,
            snapshotted_data.dbt_scd_id

        from updates_source_data as source_data
        join snapshotted_data on snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key = source_data.dbt_unique_key
        where (
            {{ strategy.row_changed }}
        )
    )

    {%- if strategy.invalidate_hard_deletes -%}
    ,

    deletes as (
    
        select
            'delete' as dbt_change_type,
            source_data.*,
            {{ snapshot_get_time() }} as dbt_valid_from,
            {{ snapshot_get_time() }} as dbt_updated_at,
            {{ snapshot_get_time() }} as dbt_valid_to,
            snapshotted_data.dbt_scd_id
    
        from snapshotted_data
        left join deletes_source_data as source_data on snapshotted_data.dbt_unique_key = source_data.dbt_unique_key
        where source_data.dbt_unique_key is null
    )
    {%- endif %}

    select * from insertions
    union all
    select * from updates
    {%- if strategy.invalidate_hard_deletes %}
    union all
    select * from deletes
    {%- endif %}

{%- endmacro %}

myco_edp.src_app_analytics.name: src_app_analytics
myco_edp.src_app_analytics.block_contents:
# What is Firebase Analytics?
As most mobile apps do, this app too, was linked to Firebase for event tracking. Firebase provides a real-time database and backend as a service. The service provides application developers an API that allows application data to be synchronized across clients (users basically) and stored on Firebase’s cloud.

References:

- [Cubert Source Page] (https://mycoco.atlassian.net/l/c/Trco0inB)
- [Sample Report] (https://datastudio.google.com/reporting/f5509bdd-8f45-44c3-87d9-2db176a2a089/page/79rOC)
- [GA Event and parameter details](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/7061705?hl=en)
- [GA Automatic Events](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/9234069?visit_id=637590390874971668-994878208&rd=1)
- [Google Analytics for FireBase](https://firebase.google.com/docs/analytics)
- [Link BigQuery to Firebase](https://support.google.com/firebase/answer/6318765#zippy=%2Cin-this-article)

# What do we mean by events?
As Google’s documentation says, events provide insight on what is happening in your app, such as user actions, system events, or errors.

## Automatic Events
There are some events (and user properties) that Firebase tracks automatically, for example the event of app first opened after it was installed, the notification dismissed event, the OS update or an app update event etc.

## Custom Events
Apart from the automatic events, developers can create their own events to track user activity or system events/errors. There is no limit on the total volume of events your app logs. For example, if you’re a dating app, you can choose to record an event when a user swipes right on someone, or when a user changes their search preferences from a radius of 5km to 500km. If you’re an e-commerce app, you can choose to record an event when user adds/removes an item from cart or when a user checks out etc.

## Event Logging
With each event, firebase lets you track a bunch of other relevant information. They can be grouped as event property (screen from which the event originated, which user ID was swiped right, what new search distance was set etc.), user property (user ID , their age, gender etc.), device property (device type, brand, model, OS, language etc.), location property (where was the event originated country, region, city etc.), app property (app version, store through which the app was installed) etc.

# Best Practices
After you start creating reports using exported data in BigQuery, you’ll encounter discrepancy between Analytics and your reports. You can use the following best practices to get better results:
1. Use the table for a specific day instead of using a date comparison on the event timestamp in the WHERE clause. The event timestamp is based on client device time, which is often skewed.
2. Never compare events like session_start or user_engagement that are triggered quite often.
3. If any query runs out of allocated resources, remove the ORDER BY clause since it is a quite expensive operation and cannot be processed in parallel so try to avoid it (or try applying it in a limited result set)
4. Always get a report past 3 days so that the data exported from GA4F to BigQuery gets normalized. Data is only exported to BigQuery once per day. And so, queries which include the most recent three days will show different results between Firebase Analytics and BigQuery.

dbt.__overview__.name: __overview__
dbt.__overview__.block_contents:
### Welcome!

Welcome to the auto-generated documentation for your dbt project!

### Navigation

You can use the `Project` and `Database` navigation tabs on the left side of the window to explore the models
in your project.

#### Project Tab
The `Project` tab mirrors the directory structure of your dbt project. In this tab, you can see all of the
models defined in your dbt project, as well as models imported from dbt packages.

#### Database Tab
The `Database` tab also exposes your models, but in a format that looks more like a database explorer. This view
shows relations (tables and views) grouped into database schemas. Note that ephemeral models are _not_ shown
in this interface, as they do not exist in the database.

### Graph Exploration
You can click the blue icon on the bottom-right corner of the page to view the lineage graph of your models.

On model pages, you'll see the immediate parents and children of the model you're exploring. By clicking the `Expand`
button at the top-right of this lineage pane, you'll be able to see all of the models that are used to build,
or are built from, the model you're exploring.

Once expanded, you'll be able to use the `--models` and `--exclude` model selection syntax to filter the
models in the graph. For more information on model selection, check out the [dbt docs](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/model-selection-syntax).

Note that you can also right-click on models to interactively filter and explore the graph.

---

### More information

- [What is dbt](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/overview)?
- Read the [dbt viewpoint](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/viewpoint)
- [Installation](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/installation)
- Join the [chat](https://community.getdbt.com/) on Slack for live questions and support.
